title: Air-fryer Cajun wedges
subtitle: Spiced potato wedges with a crisp edge
excerpt: Potato wedges tossed in a Cajun spice mix and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 22 to 25 minutes. No parboiling required: the wedge shape has enough surface area that the air fryer heat penetrates to the centre before the outside over-browns.
sourceNotes: Cajun-spiced potato wedges became a British pub and restaurant staple in the 1990s through the popularity of Cajun and Creole cuisine. The spice mix uses smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cayenne, and thyme, reflecting the Louisiana cooking tradition. The wedge shape is better suited to air frying than chips because the thicker cross-section cooks through before the surface burns.

content.text: Leave the skin on. The skin of a Maris Piper wedge provides structural integrity, helps the spice rub adhere, and crisps to a satisfying texture. Peeling wedges before air frying removes the layer that carries most of the flavour and produces a softer result.

items.name: Potato
items.prepNote: Maris Piper, scrubbed, cut into wedges (each potato into 8 wedges)

content.text: Pat the {{potato}} wedges dry with kitchen paper. Mix the {{smoked-paprika}}, {{garlic-powder}}, {{onion-powder}}, {{cayenne-pepper}}, {{thyme}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} into a spice blend. Toss the wedges with {{olive-oil}} until coated, then add the spice mix and toss again until evenly covered. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Arrange in a single layer, cut-side down where possible. Cook at 200°C for 22 to 25 minutes, turning once at the 12-minute mark, until the skin is crisp and the flesh is completely tender when pierced with a knife.

items.fix: Pat the wedges dry before adding oil. Use the stated amount: too much oil makes the wedge soft and greasy rather than crisp
items.cause: Potatoes not dried before oiling, or too much oil
items.symptom: Wedges cooked but soft, not crisp

items.fix: Cut to 8 wedges per potato (roughly 2.5 cm at the widest point). Reduce to 190°C if the spices are darkening before the 12-minute mark
items.cause: Wedges too thick, or air fryer running very hot
items.symptom: Spice coating burning on the outside before potato is cooked through

items.fix: Try to cut wedges as uniformly as possible. If some are noticeably smaller, remove them earlier. Turn at the 12-minute mark to ensure both cut surfaces get direct heat
items.cause: Different sizes from inconsistent cutting, or basket not turned
items.symptom: Wedges unevenly cooked, some soft and some crisp

content.text: Cajun wedges are a pub classic that works as a side for burgers, grilled chicken, or ribs, or as a sharing snack with a sour cream and chive dip. They are more substantial than chips and hold their temperature better, making them a good choice when the rest of the meal needs a few minutes of preparation after the wedges come out.
